title: Ashes of the Singularity: Escalation

notes: Fails to load launcher.
title: Ashes of the Singularity: Escalation

notes:
The Game starts form the Stardock-launcher. The Launcher has an option to reset all settings. I did this before trying the game. 
Only DirectX 11 (witch is selected as the standard api) works. DirectX 12 is not available in the settings . Vulkan can be selected but causes the game to crash at the restart that is needed to apply the settings. I had to reset my pc and  had to reset the game settings with the stardock launcher to make it work again. 

I did some benchmarking and played a short skirmish match. Performance was good ( about stable 48 fps) at high quality graphic preset.
title: Ashes of the Singularity: Escalation

notes: Played the first two campaign levels with no issues. Seems to run like native.
title: Ashes of the Singularity: Escalation

notes: Flawless. Tested at 3440x1440 resolution. Tough sound was a bit... loud, just a little strange
title: Ashes of the Singularity: Escalation

notes:
launcher appears, however if you press either play, or play in safe mode, launcher disappears and game crashes without showing.
my first sign of a possible culprit was when i tried to force it to vulkan output, i clicked edit settings file in the launcher, and notepad (wine) shows up... unable to find file... 
you can SAVE the file, but it still fails to appear.
I tried copying my WINDOWS data for the game into the compat data equivilant, again same problem.
in this case, my summary is it can't read the files due to some pathing thing, and has no settings, causing the engine to crash.
that said, i'm running fedora, and i've seen something about this, but no actual how to fixes. 
I don't think i tried advanced fixes (it was the first game i messed with, still figuring stuff out)
title: Ashes of the Singularity: Escalation

notes: The 3D sound seems to not work accurately
title: Ashes of the Singularity: Escalation

notes:
Technically the launcher opens but it is just a ghosted window of what ever was on the screen at the time with no actual graphics. If you press the super key you can see that it is rendered correctly but selecting it goes back to the non-rendered window.

You can guess where the play button is and it will respond but steam just shows "running" for about 3 seconds and terminates. As people are reporting some success on Arch and Ubuntu this might be a specific issue to Fedora?

I can get much further using wine staging with DXVK put the game will not progress past the "Turinium Optimised" loading screen
title: Ashes of the Singularity: Escalation

notes:
I made a report yesterday saying that this game is unplayable. I decided to completely un-install and re-download the entire game again to see if it made any difference. To my surprise the game actually run first time.

The Launcher is still an untextured window for me. You have to press the Super Key (Windows button) on the keyboard to see that it is open. Once you guess where the play button is the game will launch. (The best way I have found to do this is to open a web browser on your primary monitor, start Ashes Launcher and then minimise the web browser. The outline of the window will become apparent).

On my machine the game will only run if I have "Disable Esync" set. Without this steam says "running" for 3 seconds and then stops.

With my machine I have set the graphics settings to "High" @ 1920x1080 and run the in game benchmark to give you an idea of how it runs.

The benchmark gives an average FPS of 76.3 with a peak of 97 FPS.

Definitely playable. All multiplayer options are greyed out so single player is the only option at the moment but this is a very promising start.
title: Ashes of the Singularity: Escalation

notes: You need to disable Esync in order to have it working. You will also need to guess where the launch button is placed since the launcher window is invisible (use ALT+TAB to select it).
title: Ashes of the Singularity: Escalation

notes: Launcher is invisible, and there is some graphics setting which when enabled causes the game to crash on load. Not that the latter matters since anything other than the lowest setting is too slow to play (Vulkan and DX11 run at about the same speed). I ran a benchmark just fine, and played part of the tutorial, but loading a save results in a crash, making it unplayable.

notes: Have had to skip the launcher as it errors about not finding a file (gamedata.dat). Multiplayer completely refuses to work. Audio (XAudio) is also bit glitchy.

title: Ashes of the Singularity: Escalation

notes:
did not try tweaks (forgot), that said, nothing appears to have changed from the underlying problem i suspected the first time, no file save means the settings file is EMPTY/inaccessible and the game crashes from invalid data...
i've not crosschecked with windows (as i've not got it installed under windows anymore). just a stab in the dark suspicion.
i'm running fedora as well.
title: Ashes of the Singularity: Escalation

notes:
okay, apparently disregard my previous posts... vulkan was not activating, and hence, no DX11 capability.
i'm not giving it a platinum rating because VULKAN still won't work. which i think is actually proton's fault here. (or maybe it's something else i don't know...)
game ran as well as windows otherwise, had a massive match (13 insane AI) ended up leaving match when each of the remaining three ai fielded around 300k armies...
by then, this poor card was well below 10fps... (under windows it ran like a bag anyway. card is not quite upto it)
